reasons: All 7 REFINE actions from iteration 0 have been completed: continuous metrics added (P1), hypotheses H4/H9 revised, PCA-reduced whitening designed (P5), proposal updated with Evidence-Driven Revisions, task_plan.json fully redesigned with 12 tasks across 5 phases, toxicity framed as task-type boundary (RQ3 new), planner re-run with parallelization plan

FM1 spectral diagnosis has strong Tier 1 evidence: gradient r_eff=10 (top-5 captures 85.6% variance) vs representation r_eff=63 (34.9%), RepSim > TRAK by +32pp (counterfact) and +17pp (ftrace), H6 K-FAC control confirmed (+17.4pp gap)
Task-type boundary discovery is a genuine new contribution: toxicity reversal (-24pp) is systematic and mechanistically explained (gradient norm artifact, Cohen's d=2.66)

Remaining uncertainties (FM2 detectability, whitening effectiveness, gap decomposition, retrieval boundary) are empirical questions that require experimental data -- no further refinement without new data would resolve them

Task plan has 4 explicit decision gates (after P1, P3, P4, P5) enabling graceful scope narrowing: paper is viable under FM2-fails, retrieval-matches, whitening-fails, and gap-doesnt-decompose scenarios
Infrastructure fully validated: all 8+ methods functional, pipeline tested (28 min pilot), 4x RTX 4090 available, ~4h wall-clock with 4-GPU parallelism
next_actions: Execute full-scale experiments following task_plan.json wave parallelization: Wave 1 (P1 continuous metrics + P2 eigenspectrum + P3 retrieval, 60 min), Wave 2 (P1 injection + P4 layer sweep + P2 TRAK sweep, 60 min), Wave 3 (P2 RepSim sweep + P4 cosine/gap, 45 min), Wave 4 (P5 PCA whitening, 45 min), Wave 5 (final analysis, 15 min)
Evaluate P1 decision gate after FM2 verification: if Kendall tau gain >= 0.05 AND contamination injection recovery >= 90%, FM2 is Tier 1; otherwise demote to theoretical hypothesis
Evaluate P3 decision gate after retrieval baselines: if Contriever/GTR < 3pp gap with RepSim on 2+ tasks, reposition paper
Evaluate P4 decision gate after gap decomposition: if combined TRAK-PCA closes to within 10pp of RepSim, FM1 is dominant mechanism
Evaluate P5 decision gate after PCA whitening: if any k outperforms RepSim by >= 3pp, framework is prescriptive
Proceed to result_debate stage after final_analysis task completes

backup_candidates.promotion_criterion: PCA-whitened attribution outperforms RepSim by >= 5pp on >= 2 tasks
Contriever/GTR matches RepSim (< 3pp gap) on >= 2 tasks

increase_reasons: +5: All REFINE actions completed, no remaining methodology gaps
+5: Robust decision gates built into experimental plan
+3: New H10 (gap decomposition) and H11 (contamination injection) add high-probability contributions
+2: Validated infrastructure and realistic wall-clock estimates

fm2_undetectable.mitigation: P1 decision gate; contamination injection as independent validation

retrieval_matches_repsim.impact: Reposition from attribution quality to attribution-vs-retrieval boundary
retrieval_matches_repsim.mitigation: P3 decision gate; cand_d promotion path

pca_whitening_fails.impact: Framework is taxonomic only, not prescriptive
pca_whitening_fails.mitigation: P5 decision gate; honest negative result

risk_assessment.worst_case_outcome: FM1 spectral diagnosis + task-type boundary + systematic benchmark (weak poster)

publication_viability.best_case: NeurIPS 2026 spotlight (FM2 validated + whitening works, P=0.25)
publication_viability.base_case: NeurIPS 2026 poster (FM2 partially works OR whitening marginal, P=0.50)
publication_viability.worst_case: Weak poster / workshop (all Tier 2 experiments fail, P=0.02)
